subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Eight friends, K, L, M, N, O, P, Q and R, are sitting at two circular tables, one smaller inner table
and one bigger outer table. Both the circular tables have the same centre. Four persons are
sitting at each table at equal distances between them, facing the centre. M is to the
immediate right of L. Q and N are sitting in the south and are facing R and L. M is sitting
opposite to O at the same table. P is sitting opposite K at the outer table. N is between P and
K at the same table. L is between M and O at the same table. Who is sitting in the north at the
outer table?
q:
Eight friends, K, L, M, N, O, P, Q and R, are sitting at two circular tables, one smaller inner table
and one bigger outer table. Both the circular tables have the same centre. Four persons are
sitting at each table at equal distances between them, facing the centre. M is to the
immediate right of L. Q and N are sitting in the south and are facing R and L. M is sitting
opposite to O at the same table. P is sitting opposite K at the outer table. N is between P and
K at the same table. L is between M and O at the same table. Who is sitting in the north at the
outer table?
explanation: Gold (atomic weight ~197 g/mol, density 19.3 g/cm^3) is the densest/heaviest metal among Gold, Silver, Iron, and Copper.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in
the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts,
decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.
Statements: 
All rats are dogs.
Some rats are hens.
Conclusions:
I. Some rats are dogs.
II. Some hens are rats.
q:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in
the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts,
decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.
Statements: 
All rats are dogs.
Some rats are hens.
Conclusions:
I. Some rats are dogs.
II. Some hens are rats.

options.text: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow

explanation: $Squaring both sides \text{ of } x + 1/x = 5 gives (x + 1/x)^2 = 25 ⇒ x^2 + 2 + 1/x^2 = 25 ⇒ x^2 + 1/x^2 = 23.$

q: A sum of ₹1,727 is divided into three parts A, B and C so that their amounts after 3, 4 and 5 years, respectively, are equal, the rate of simple interest being 5% per annum. How much is A?
explanation: Solving the mathematical equation for this Simple and Compound Interest question yields Option D as the correct result.

subject: English Language
question:
Comprehension:
In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.

Just as the correction and refinement of knowledge takes place through the (1)______ of science, in the same way the (2)______ of human nature is done through arts. By making only the progress and refinement of knowledge his aim, man's (3)______ powers have increased a lot, but by (4)______ the arts, we are getting only their burning sensation. Artlessness makes man inert, and indeed today man is falling prey to his own (5)______ from all sides.

SubQuestion No : 1
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.
